summary: Verified Login Phone-OTP → Firebase migration. Found and fixed a critical .env formatting bug that was defeating the migration. After fix, all acceptance criteria met.

verifications.backend_firebase_verify_bogus_token: 401 with detail 'Invalid Firebase ID token: Wrong number of segments in token: b\'bogus_token_xyz\''
verifications.backend_firebase_admin_initialised: INFO gemora Firebase Admin initialised for project doctorappointmentcx (present in backend logs)
verifications.console_errors_login_page: 0 app errors (only a benign 'requestStorageAccess: Permission denied' from Google reCAPTCHA iframe in headless browser)

root_cause_and_fix.issue: On first test run, clicking 'Phone OTP' on /login STILL called the mock /api/auth/otp/send and displayed dev code 123456 (bug appeared unfixed).
root_cause_and_fix.rca: frontend/.env line had 'ENABLE_HEALTH_CHECK=false' and 'REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY=AIzaSy...' concatenated with no newline separator between them. Create-React-App's dotenv parser therefore interpreted REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY as part of the value of ENABLE_HEALTH_CHECK, so REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY was undefined at build time. FIREBASE_ENABLED (= !!(apiKey && projectId && appId)) evaluated to false, and PhoneVerify.jsx fell back to the legacy /api/auth/otp/send mock path.
root_cause_and_fix.fix_applied: Inserted a newline between ENABLE_HEALTH_CHECK=false and REACT_APP_FIREBASE_API_KEY=... in /app/frontend/.env, then restarted the frontend supervisor to pick up the new env.
root_cause_and_fix.post_fix_evidence: 21 network calls to identitytoolkit.googleapis.com / recaptcha / gstatic; 0 calls to /api/auth/otp/send; reCAPTCHA visual challenge (stairs) rendered on-screen; no dev-code toast.

critical_code_review_comments: Guard against .env parsing bugs — add a runtime assertion in /app/frontend/src/lib/firebase.js that warns if FIREBASE_ENABLED is false but any REACT_APP_FIREBASE_* var is set (helps catch env misconfig fast).
Consider removing the legacy /api/auth/otp/send fallback in PhoneVerify.jsx once Firebase is deemed the only supported path — the fallback silently hides misconfigurations like the one found above.

Backend error message 'Invalid Firebase ID token: Wrong number of segments in token: b\'bogus_token_xyz\'' leaks a Python bytes-repr. Consider sanitising the upstream exception before returning to clients.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login Phone OTP now correctly uses PhoneVerify + Firebase reCAPTCHA. The only change required to make it work was fixing a missing newline in frontend/.env. Password login and signup PhoneVerify were unaffected. Playwright cannot solve Firebase reCAPTCHA so a full SMS delivery e2e is not possible in this env — that is expected.
